description:
Letter from the Master of the Shipmasts to a Nubian eparch in Ibrīm. The letter reports on the activities of Merki, a man who was in the service of the Nubian eparch and the Master of the Shipmasts. Khan writes that Merki seems to be "working with the Muslim writer of the letter to supply the eparch with military equipment but he has also taken upon himself the responsibility of operating as an agent of the Nubian authorities in the suppression of a rebellion" (Khan, 255). PG. 

Alternate readings (ASE): 
- For وانا in l. 15 it is possible to read وانما ("only"), as in the following line. 
- For انهيه in l. 17 it is possible to read انهبه ("plunder him"), as in l. 13. 
- For خيلك in l. 19 it is possible to read ذلك ("that"). 
- For the translation of ḥadīth in l. 16, this can be understood as a synonym for kalām (repeating the idea of "what he says is not old"). 

Khan, Qasr Ibrīm, doc. 23.
description_he: מכתב ממאסטר תורני האניה לאפרכוס הנובי, מדווח על פעילות מרכי שסיפק ציוד צבאי ופעל לדיכוי מרד בשם הרשויות הנוביות, מגוף קצר איברים
